title: Cyimarra

{{Quote|Rumors regarding the secret Grand Display are running wild, and many prognosticators are predicting a re-creation of Cyimarra's Crystal Spires, since it is known that both worlds ''[Corellia and Corulag]'' sent delegations to Cyimarra last season.|A news report by Coruscant Daily NewsFeed|<ref name="GNN">{{JournalCite|5|Galaxywide NewsNets}}</ref>}}
'''Cyimarra''' was a terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim's]] [[Senex sector/Legends|Senex sector]]. The [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the the [[Noble/Legends|aristocratic]] [[House Araba]], its surface featured [[City/Legends|cities]] interspersed among [[Forest/Legends|woodlands]] and [[Mountain/Legends|mountains]] as well as a [[Ocean/Legends|sea]].

Cyimarra was famous for the [[Crystal Spires]], a series of ancient towers of uniform height that were evenly spaced across the planet's surface. By [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], the planets [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]] and [[Corulag/Legends|Corulag]] sent delegations to Cyimarra, and it was speculated that they would present a recreation of the Crystal Spires on the planet [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] during the [[Galactic Fair|festivities]] of that [[Standard year/Legends|year's]] [[New Year Fete Week/Legends|New Year Fete Week]].

==Description==
Cyimarra was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Cyimarra system/Legends|Cyimarra system]] of the [[Senex sector/Legends|Senex sector]], a part of the [[Senex-Juvex/Legends|Senex-Juvex]] region in the [[Western Reaches/Legends|Western Reaches]] portion of the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]]. The planet lay between the [[Mussubir system/Legends|Mussubir]] and [[Veron system/Legends|Veron systems]] on the [[Senex-Juvex Loop]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]], and another [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] route linked it to the [[Skartis system/Legends|Skartis system]]. Cyimarra was a quiet, terrestrial world whose surface featured [[Forest/Legends|woodlands]], towering [[Mountain/Legends|mountains]], [[Water/Legends|icy]] crags, and a [[Ocean/Legends|sea]] at least several [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] in depth.<ref name="TEA" />

==History==
{{Quote|Corellia and Corulag are co-hosting the Fair displays this year, and will unveil their Grand Display tomorrow.|A news report by Coruscant Daily NewsFeed|<ref name="GNN" />}}
At some point by [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]],<ref name="Date">The [[in-universe]] news report mentioning Cyimarra in {{JournalCite|5|Galaxywide NewsNets}} is set on the date 36:F1:1, which corresponds to [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], per the reasoning [[Template:YearMathGRS|here]].</ref> the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] planets<ref name="TEA" /> [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]] and [[Corulag/Legends|Corulag]] both sent delegations to Cyimarra. During the following season, Corellia and Corulag were co-hosting the [[Standard year/Legends|annual]] [[Galactic Fair|Imperial Fair]] displays of the [[New Year Fete Week/Legends|New Year Fete Week]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]],<ref name="GNN" /> the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] planet of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] located in the Core Worlds.<ref name="TEA" /> A [[Standard day/Legends|day]] before the unveiling of the secret [[Grand Display]], many prognosticators predicted that Corellia and Corulag would present a recreation of the [[Crystal Spires]] of Cyimarra, a hypothesis which was mentioned on the [[Great ReSynchronization|date]] 36:F1:1 in a news report by the [[Coruscant Daily Newsfeed/Legends|Coruscant Daily Newsfeed]].<ref name="GNN" />

==Inhabitants==
Cyimarra was the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the [[Noble/Legends|aristocratic]] [[House Araba]].<ref name="TEA" />

==Locations==
Although Cyimarra had graceful [[City/Legends|cities]] interspersed among its various forms of terrain, the feature the planet was famous for was the Crystal Spires. A series of unimaginably ancient, glittering towers of uniform height that girdled the planet, the Crystal Spires were placed across the world in such a way that the distance between any two neighboring structures was exactly the same—despite some of the towers being located atop high crags and others being submerged deep underwater.<ref name="TEA" />

==Behind the scenes==
Cyimarra was first mentioned in the "[[Galaxywide NewsNets]]" feature of [[West End Games]]' [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 5|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 5]], which was published in [[February]] [[1995]]. The feature was authored by [[Paul Sudlow]].<ref name="GNN" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the planet in grid square L-17.<ref name="TEA" />
